title: Gate the promotion through the approve marker and launch real work
description: Implement `bober do <findingId>` (without --dry-run): resolve the promoter and build the PromotionPlan, write a .bober/approvals/promote-<findingId>.pending.json marker summarizing the plan, gate on it (interactive prompts confirm in a TTY; wait for an external `bober approve`/`bober reject` in non-TTY; `--yes` auto-approves), and on approval launch the work through an injected Launcher port whose default adapter wraps the chat RunSpawner to spawn a detached `agent-bober run <task> --run-id <id>`. On launch, set Finding.promotesTo = {kind:'bober-run', runId, launchedAt, status:'launched'} and transition the Finding status open->in-progress via the FindingStore port. On rejection, delete the pending marker and leave the Finding unchanged.

successCriteria.description: `npm run build` (tsc) finishes with zero type errors after the Launcher port, the RunSpawner-backed adapter, and the approve-gate wiring are added.

With a fake Launcher and a confirm stub returning approve, `bober do <id>` writes promote-<id>.pending.json, invokes launcher.launch exactly once with the promoter's task, then writes the .approved marker and deletes the pending marker.

After an approved promotion the FindingStore fake shows the finding status equals 'in-progress' and promotesTo.runId equals the runId returned by the fake Launcher with promotesTo.status equals 'launched'.
When the gate resolves to reject, launcher.launch is invoked zero times, the finding status stays at 'open', and the promote-<id>.pending.json marker is removed.
In non-TTY mode `bober do <id>` writes the pending marker and waits; an external `bober approve promote-<id>` (reusing src/state/approval-state.ts) resolves it and the launch then proceeds.

nonGoals: Do not invent a new approval storage format - reuse savePending/readPending/saveApproved/saveRejected/deletePending from src/state/approval-state.ts.
Do not run the pipeline in-process (no blocking runPipeline await) - launch detached via the Launcher port.
Do not mark the finding 'done' here - terminal reconciliation is Sprint 3.
Do not add a fleet Launcher implementation - ship only the RunSpawner-backed default adapter and keep the port open.
stopConditions: `npm run build` passes and `npm test -- src/do-bridge` shows the approve path launches once and links the runId while the reject path launches zero times and leaves the finding at status 'open'.
definitionOfDone: Running `bober do <findingId>` surfaces the planned launch, waits for an explicit approve/reject through the same .bober/approvals markers the run pipeline uses, and only on approval spawns a detached `agent-bober run` for the coding task. The promoted Finding then carries a promotesTo ref linking the new runId with status 'launched' and its status is 'in-progress'; a rejected promotion spawns nothing and leaves the Finding untouched.
assumptions: The detached launcher reuses src/chat/run-spawner.ts RunSpawner.spawn (line 97), which shells `agent-bober run <task> --run-id <id>` (run-spawner.ts:113) -> runRunCommand (src/cli/commands/run.ts:181) -> runPipeline (src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts:970).

The approve gate reuses src/state/approval-state.ts markers (savePending:49, saveApproved:106, saveRejected:122, deletePending:138); non-TTY waiting mirrors the poll loop in src/orchestrator/checkpoints/mechanisms/disk.ts.
FindingStore from Sprint 1 is extended with an update method (e.g. setPromotion/updateFinding) to persist promotesTo and the status transition.
outOfScope: Medical/financial promoters and a fleet Launcher.
generatorNotes: Launcher port: interface { launch(plan: PromotionPlan): Promise<{ runId: string; pid?: number }> }; the default adapter constructs RunSpawner({ projectRoot, sessionId }) and calls spawn(plan.task, runId) (src/chat/run-spawner.ts:97). Generate runId like `do-<findingId>-<timestamp>`. Gate: write the pending marker via savePending (src/state/approval-state.ts:49) with checkpointId `promote-<findingId>` and a prompt summarizing plan.task; if process.stdout.isTTY and not --yes, use prompts() to confirm then write saveApproved/saveRejected; otherwise poll readPending->approved/rejected (mirror src/orchestrator/checkpoints/mechanisms/disk.ts request loop) until resolved or a timeout; --yes short-circuits to approved. After approval call launcher.launch, then update the finding via the FindingStore port (extend it this sprint). On reject, deletePending and return without mutation. Handler never throws -> set process.exitCode (memory.ts pattern). Inject the Launcher and the confirm function so unit tests use fakes and never spawn a real process.
evaluatorNotes: Verify approve -> launch-once -> link (runId + status 'in-progress' + promotesTo.status 'launched'); reject -> no launch -> no mutation -> pending marker removed; `--yes` bypasses the prompt but still writes and clears the marker. Confirm only src/state/approval-state.ts is used for approvals (no new format) and that the Launcher is injected (no real execa in unit tests).
